elections.note: Certified final (Total Voters) = 650,545 (precinct 194,606 + VBM 455,939), 62.54% of registration. No election-night first-report count sourceable: the precise ~8pm first-tranche total lived only on the overwritten live-results page (Wayback blocked here); only the final summary PDF is archived at docs.voteinfo.net, and no news/press release quoted a pre-Election-Day VBM return count for 2018. Pre-epollbook (adopted 2022), pre-ASV (adopted 2025) baseline.

Numerator is a PROXY for the first 8:15pm tranche: 645,799 vote-by-mail/early ballots cast of 1,243,154 issued as of Election-Day morning (Patch, posted Nov 3 2020 9:41am). The registrar stated 'ballots turned in before Monday will be the first results released after polls close,' so this pre-ED VBM count is essentially what the first report contained (actual 8:15pm tranche marginally higher). 645,799/1,016,896 = 63.5%. Certified final (Total Voters) = 1,016,896 (precinct 109,028 + VBM 907,868). Pre-epollbook, pre-ASV.

Numerator is the END-OF-ELECTION-NIGHT total, not the 8:20pm first tranche: registrar reported 'election night vote counting is complete' with ~205,000 ballots counted, with nearly 325,000 VBM + ~10,000 provisional still to count (Idyllwild Town Crier, Nov 9 2022). The 8:20pm first-tranche figure (pre-ED VBM only) would be lower and was not separately sourceable. 205,000/604,617 = 33.9% (upper bound on the first-report share). Low-turnout midterm. Certified final (Total Voters) = 604,617 (precinct 59,812 + VBM 544,805). First November general AFTER e-pollbook adoption (2022); still pre-ASV.

Numerator is a LOWER-BOUND PROXY for the first 8:20pm tranche: 526,353 VBM ballots 'returned good' of 1,445,849 issued as of Monday Nov 4 (day before E-Day), per registrar via Patch. The 8:20pm first report (pre-ED VBM, which kept growing Mon afternoon/Tue) is somewhat higher than this day-before figure. 526,353/959,098 = 54.9%. Certified final (Total Voters) = 959,098 (precinct 170,437 + VBM 788,661), 69.88% turnout. Post-epollbook, pre-ASV. Cleanest e-pollbook pre/post comparison is 2020 (63.5%) vs this 2024 presidential — but note the 2020 proxy is as-of E-Day-morning while 2024 is as-of day-before, and the first-tranche share is driven mainly by how early voters return VBM, not by e-pollbooks (which speed in-person/vote-center processing).

Statewide SPECIAL election (single ballot measure, Prop 50 congressional redistricting) - not a general; turnout dynamics differ, so not directly comparable to the presidential/midterm rows. This is the only election AFTER ASV adoption (2025). Numerator is a first-tranche PROXY: ~390,035 VBM 'returned good' of ~1,459,482 issued as of Monday Nov 3 (day before E-Day), per registrar. 390,035/657,322 = 59.3%. For reference, the END-OF-NIGHT tally on Prop 50 was ~419,450 votes with all precincts reporting (238,494 yes + 180,956 no), ~63.8% of certified, per Murrieta Patch (https://patch.com/california/murrieta/riverside-county-votes-yes-prop-50-early-tallies-show). Certified final (Total Voters) = 657,322 (precinct 57,343 + VBM 599,979). Post-epollbook, post-ASV.
